meta.title: My Journey
meta.subtitle: From learning fundamentals of computer engineering to building real-world backend systems and cloud-deployed applications.
meta.description: A detailed chronological journey covering my education, continuous learning, professional growth, challenges, transitions, and achievements as a software engineer.

overview.headline: Learning, Building, and Evolving Through Experience
overview.summary: My journey is a continuous process of learning, experimenting, failing, improving, and building. Starting from academic foundations, I gradually transitioned into real-world software development, focusing heavily on backend systems, databases, authentication, and cloud infrastructure while maintaining a strong full stack mindset.
overview.currentFocus: Scalable backend systems, system design fundamentals, and cloud-native architecture
overview.longTermGoal: To grow into a senior backend and system design engineer capable of designing distributed systems that are reliable, secure, and performance-oriented at scale.

custom.inspiration: Solving real problems through clean engineering

timeline.title: Initial Interest in Computers and Technology
timeline.subtitle: Curiosity-driven exploration phase
timeline.description: This phase marked the beginning of my interest in computers, where I started exploring how software, operating systems, and basic programming concepts worked out of pure curiosity.
activities: Explored basic computer operations and system usage
Learned fundamentals of logic and problem solving informally

achievements.title: Built First Logical Programs
achievements.description: Successfully wrote simple logic-based programs that strengthened my interest in programming.

reflection.challenges: Lack of structured guidance and resources.
reflection.learnings: Curiosity is a strong driver for long-term learning.

timeline.title: Started Diploma in Computer Engineering
timeline.subtitle: Foundation of programming and computer science
timeline.description: This phase established my academic foundation in computer engineering, covering programming, databases, operating systems, and networking concepts.
activities: Studied DBMS, Operating Systems, Computer Networks, and OOP concepts
Worked on academic mini-projects and lab assignments

achievements.title: Graduated with Distinction
achievements.description: Completed diploma education with strong academic performance and hands-on understanding.

reflection.challenges: Understanding abstract computer science concepts initially.
reflection.learnings: Strong fundamentals are non-negotiable for growth.

timeline.title: Intensive Self-Learning and Skill Building
timeline.subtitle: Transition from academic learning to industry-oriented skills
timeline.description: Parallel to academics, I invested significant time in self-learning modern web development, backend engineering, and tooling used in the software industry.
activities: Learned JavaScript, Node.js, Express, and REST API concepts
Practiced Git, debugging, and clean coding practices

achievements.title: Built Multiple Practice Projects
achievements.description: Completed several self-initiated projects to solidify understanding of backend and frontend integration.

reflection.challenges: Managing time between academics and self-learning.
reflection.learnings: Consistency compounds faster than short bursts of effort.

timeline.title: Professional Software Engineering Work
timeline.subtitle: Real-world backend systems and cloud deployments
timeline.description: Currently working on real-world software systems focusing on backend APIs, database architecture, authentication mechanisms, and AWS-based deployments.
activities: Designed scalable REST APIs and backend services
Handled cloud infrastructure, security, and deployment workflows

achievements.title: Delivered Production-Ready Systems
achievements.description: Successfully delivered backend-heavy applications with secure authentication and optimized performance.

reflection.challenges: Taking ownership of end-to-end system reliability.
reflection.learnings: Designing for scale requires foresight and discipline.
